summary: Maintainer-filed tracking issue to standardize SKTouchEventArgs.WheelDelta across all platforms to use the v120 convention (120 units = one discrete mouse wheel notch), with seven sub-issues covering Blazor, Android, macOS, iOS, Tizen, WPF/WinForms, and GTK.

analysis.summary: This is a cross-platform enhancement tracking issue filed by the maintainer (mattleibow). It defines the v120 standard for WheelDelta normalization and decomposes the work into seven platform-specific sub-issues. Code investigation confirms that only Windows MAUI passes a real WheelDelta (from PointerPointProperties.MouseWheelDelta), while Android hardcodes 0, Apple/iOS uses the basic constructor (no wheel param), Tizen uses the basic constructor (no wheel param), and Blazor/WPF/WinForms/GTK have no wheel event handling at all.
analysis.rationale: Classified as type/enhancement because it improves existing WheelDelta behavior rather than fixing a crash or broken output. Area is SkiaSharp.Views because WheelDelta lives in SKTouchEventArgs and all work is in platform-specific touch/view handlers. No platform labels since this is cross-platform by nature — individual sub-issues carry platform labels.

keySignals.text: SKTouchEventArgs.WheelDelta currently has no documented unit contract
keySignals.interpretation: The property exists but lacks a formal specification for what values mean.

keySignals.text: Only Windows MAUI passes meaningful values (raw ±120 per notch)
keySignals.interpretation: Confirmed by code: Windows SKTouchHandler.cs line 122 reads MouseWheelDelta which follows v120.

keySignals.text: All other platforms either hardcode 0 or don't handle wheel events at all
keySignals.interpretation: Confirmed by code: Android passes 0, Apple/Tizen use no-wheelDelta constructors, Blazor/WPF/GTK have no wheel handling.

codeInvestigation.finding: WheelDelta is declared as 'public int WheelDelta { get; private set; }' — the property exists but has no documentation or unit contract.
Windows handler subscribes to PointerWheelChanged and reads pointerPoint.Properties.MouseWheelDelta (already v120). This is the only platform that passes a real wheelDelta value.
Android handler uses MotionEvent Touch events only. All SKTouchEventArgs are constructed with wheelDelta=0. No AXIS_VSCROLL or GenericMotionEvent handling exists.
Apple handler uses the basic SKTouchEventArgs constructor (id, actionType, point, inContact) which defaults wheelDelta to 0. No scroll/wheel gesture recognition exists.
Tizen handler uses the basic SKTouchEventArgs constructor. No WheelEvent handling exists.

actionability.reason: This is a maintainer-filed tracking issue that defines the v120 standard and coordinates seven sub-issues. It should remain open until all sub-issues are implemented.

actions.description: Update area label to SkiaSharp.Views since all work is in platform view handlers
